zh.lifeSummary: Margery Joyce Linney於1355年出生在英國藍卡爾霍爾頓 姆 哈代。她於1374年與William Edward Barlow在英國英格蘭藍卡爾曼徹斯特霍爾頓--德洛可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1412年享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: Linney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from an Old English female personal name Lindgifu, Lindgeofu, composed of the elements lind ‘lime (wood)’, i.e. ‘shield’ (a transferred sense) + gifu, geofu ‘gift’.

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

nameMeanings.name: Joyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently from the Norman male name Josce (Middle English Josse), which in turn is from Jodocus, a Latinized form of a Breton name, Iodoc, meaning ‘lord’, borne by a 7th-century Breton saint. The name was in use in England among Breton followers of William the Conqueror. However, although this was fairly common as a male given name in the Middle Ages, it had virtually died out by the 14th century. There is evidence of its use as a girl's name from the 16th century onwards in parishes with strong Puritan links, which suggests that it may have been associated with the vocabulary word joy; see Joy . It was strongly revived in the 19th century under the influence of popular fiction. It is borne by characters in Mrs Henry Wood's East Lynne ( 1861 ) and Edna Lyall's In the Golden Days ( 1885 ). Modern use may well have been influenced also by the common Irish surname derived from the medieval Norman male name. See also Joss .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Margery Joyce Linney was born in 1355, in Chorlton cum Hardy, Lancashire, England. She married William Edward Barlow in 1374, in Chorlton-on-Medlock, Manchester, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1412, at the age of 57.

ru.lifeSummary: Margery Joyce Linney родилась в 1355 г. в местности чаурлтон кум харди, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Edward Barlow в 1374 г. в местности чаурлтон-он-медлокк, манчестир, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1412 г. в возрасте 57 лет.
